transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hi, I don't think we've actually met on a call yet.
No, we haven't.
I'm big.
But it had been so long since I'd had it.
Yeah.
But I forgot.
don't know the last time I had the flu, but I heard that it is just, like, especially brutal this year.
Yeah, I think it's just been, like, it feels like everyone has it this year.
Like, it's been everywhere.
Yeah.
I don't know why.
It feels like half of all the people I know have been out at one point or another this winter.
So, fingers crossed that we're through the worst of it now.
Seriously.
Hi, Annie.
Hello.
I was just complaining because I was out with flu for a week and my voice is still cracking and, like, not quite back to normal.
So, you'll have to forgive me if I sound funny.
Like, it's been, like, two weeks now and I still sound weird.
Like, I have a frog trapped in my chest.
Oh, my gosh.
Are you feeling better besides that?
Yeah, besides that, it's, like, the cough and, like, I feel like there was a while where I felt worse than I sounded and now I'm, like...
I sound like I feel like a normal human, but my voice still just with a frog trapped in their throat.
Oh my gosh.
So were you all out, were you like on an offsite last week or you were all traveling, right?
We, yeah, we had our revenue kickoff in Phoenix.
So it was everyone across sales, marketing, CS, a bunch of other teams as well.
So, yeah.
Yeah.
And are you all, are you all remote and based all over the place or?
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
We were all in Phoenix last week, but everyone's pretty, pretty dispersed.
We have offices in New York and the Bay Area and a couple of other satellite offices, but yeah, everyone's kind of all over the place.
Are any of you guys on the East Coast buried in snow right now or?
I am.
I'm in Boston.
Oh, wow.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's funny.
So I'm in Vermont and so people keep asking me.
And we just missed it.
Like, I think we were too far north.
So we got absolutely nothing.
So it's wild seeing the pictures of people like not that far south with three feet of snow.
So yeah, we have like almost two feet.
It's crazy.
And I feel like like already the snow banks were so bad from the last storm.
And now it's just like the city doesn't know what to do with all the snow.
But at least today, it's like sunny.
So I'm like, maybe it'll start melting.
We have a long way to go.
Oh, my gosh.
Well, I'm glad at least you're here.
So you must have power.
Yes, thankfully didn't lose power.
I was honestly nervous about that yesterday, because the wind was so bad.
Like you couldn't even see outside like the windows because they were just like covered in snow from all the wind.
So it was wild.
But thankfully didn't lose power.
That's the wind.
You've got to take the small wind where you can get them.
Exactly.
That's the only way to get through winters here.
All right.
Do you know if anyone else is coming today or if I should just jump in?
I'm going to guess.
Russell's at an event, I just realized.
And then Devin is traveling to see a customer.
So it's probably just us.
So I can just jump in and I can share the recording as well if anyone wants to catch up on anything.
You know, I realized I shared the agenda, but I wanted to first jump into, I don't know if you had a chance to see, oh, not my calendar.
You don't need to see that.
The new interface that I created on Airtable, just to make it a little easier for you all to update the status.
I don't know if you had a chance to take a look at that.
So I was just going to walk through.
So I did share the link, but if you're in the main Airtable, the way you can access it is just to go to interfaces.
I, yeah.
And get them in the queue to publish this week.
Perfect.
That's amazing.
Awesome.
So yeah, so they'll focus on these and then obviously deliver the new content as usual.
But for now, we'll focus on updating these and then hopefully, you know, we can get back on our usual publishing.
Do you all, I know we'll have 10.
My thought is we just kind of, we can either publish the full 10 or kind of, I don't know if your team has any specific.
Feelings around publishing the full 10 or if you'd rather we just add kind of like an extra every week or two until we catch up.
I mean, is there any downside to publishing all 10 at the same time?
No, there's no, not at that level.
Like you're not going to get punished by the algorithm for batch publishing 10.
know, it's like once you get to like hundreds of pages.
then you're like, okay.
Yeah.
Some clients have strong feelings about it.
So I just wanted to check and make sure that's okay.
I think at this point, we're, like, kind of playing the catch-up game.
So I think the preference would be to just push all 10 of those and, like, keep things moving.
That would be what I would suggest as well.
Okay, so we'll queue those up for publishing as soon as they are ready.
The other thing I wanted to talk through was I did go through and sort of build out the collection of topics in Airtable.
So I took a look at the content prioritization plan, which is I know what we're sort of basing our content calendar on coming out of Sprint.
And just kind of identified some holes that we had based on kind of where we wanted to publish.
And so added some additional topics in some of the focus areas.
So all to just flag that in the topics interface in Airtable, you can go in and approve, reject, leave comments there on all these news.
I think I think that's
So we do have some runway.
So that doesn't have to happen today.
It doesn't have to happen this week.
I think we're okay for a little bit.
But those are there to help build out the runway.
And then I saw there were some that had low priorities that I just wanted to touch base on before we deprioritize these.
There were a few that there were like versus comparison articles that had been marked low priority.
And my thought was it was probably because they were low to no search volume, because I know we've been sort of targeting some of the higher volume keywords.
But I did just want to flag that this type of content is capturing really high intent searches that can be really valuable, even though they're low volume.
It also like really helpful for AI visibility because you're comparing to...
products to companies.
it's essentially like training LLMs how to think and talk about Cresta in this very product forward way.
And then I would also say that we're seeing across prompts that these verses and top 10 listicles are the top-sided content.
Yeah, I'm surprised that that was marked low priority because I know that we've seen so far that the competitive ones are really the ones gaining the most traction.
So yeah, I would say that should be marked a higher priority.
And then we had a few, I think in the current, like approved to start runway of topics, were also some that just didn't have a priority.
So just flagging, my guess is some of this may have happened, like, as discussions were happening with you and Ada, like some things got prioritized, some didn't.
So it might be worth just going in and looking at the priority.
And because then the other one was there was some call center scoring keywords that were marked low priority that were more QM focused.
And this was the same thing.
I think they were low volume, but I think the search intent was very problem solving and there was an opportunity to more directly showcase the automated scoring product.
So I would also think that those might be worth revisiting again and making them at least medium priority.
These, I would say, are probably high priority, honestly.
And these, I think, depending on how much you want to focus on the product, at the very least, I would think might be medium priority.
Okay.
That makes sense.
I wanted to, yeah, so I'll just leave that with you to take a look at and see if the prioritization makes sense to you.
The other thing I wanted to ask you about was just a.
Um, there were still a few, um, non, like, old, older, um, pages that had not been indexed.
Um, so I would sort of leave that up to you if you want to, um, you know, review the list and sort of decide if it was worth making those changes.
Um, this was also the list from when we didn't meet last week, so it's possible that there may be some that have indexed now.
But, but, um, some of the older posts, I think, had not, so.
Okay, because I remember Ada, like, resubmitting anything that hadn't been indexed for whatever reason, resubmitting those, whether it was, like, GrowthX posts or not GrowthX posts.
Um, so maybe there's just a delay still from that.
Um, but yeah, I think something good to keep an eye on.
Yeah.
Okay, I think that makes sense.
Um, okay.
Okay, um, the other...
Another I wanted to touch base with is images, because I think there might have been some miscommunication there.
So on our last live sync, I walked through the images to the updated pipeline.
And I think these were based on, this was not a meeting I was on yet, but between Steve and Ada and I think our designer, Katya, I think these were based on Steve's notes.

So I wasn't sure the best way, excuse me, there's the frog, to kind of loop him in and see if he had additional feedback on these, if he was the best point of contact when it came to imagery and design there.
He's definitely the best point of contact.
I just sent him a message because I know that there had been a meeting set up for them to talk.
I don't know if you've had a chance to talk with Ada and the team about it.
I don't know that the meeting actually happened.
At him so that he sees it.
I don't think he's monitoring the GrowthX channel that much.
Okay.
So that's what I'll do right after this.
I'll just drop them into a Notion doc and see if he has additional feedback and make sure it was captured in the most recent round of updates.
Okay.
In terms of other updates, so CheckThat is now live.
know that I think Aida talked to you a little bit about that.
And I think you actually were one of the first accounts that was added on there since you were a new client.
I wanted to make sure that, I don't know if you had a chance to kind of walk through on your own yet.
But, um, no, I think I, I think I may have been, I may not have been on the call that when they went over that, I don't have anything.
Should I have received something in my email to?
To get access?
You should have gotten an email.
If you didn't, I'll resend it because it's new and it just launched.
So it can be a little bit buggy.
Would it be helpful for me to do a quick walkthrough of the platform now?
Yes, that would be great.
Yes, please.
Okay.
Yeah, I don't think I have access either.
So if you could resend the email to me as well, that would be great.
I will resend it to both of you because I had a similar situation with a client where for some reason the first email did not.
I through.
So I will re-invite you right after this.
And I'll just give you kind of a brief walkthrough so you know what you're looking at.
So when you get access, you'll land here on your overview page.
So if you've used tools like Scrunch or other AI visibility tools, the general interface will probably look similar.
So you'll see your overall competitive presence as compared to...
The list of competitors here.
So this is a list that you can adjust.
So we tend to preload it with both a mix of kind of product competitors or also organic competitors, depending on what you want to traffic.
So this is something you can update.
I think you can track up to 30 here.
And you'll also see in terms of overall your position.

So where you're appearing, where Cresta's appearing in the response, the sentiment, know, whether it's positive or negative, and then, you know, share of citations for the prompts that we're tracking.
So once you actually go into your prompts, right now we're tracking 225.
I also linked to that in our agenda as a spreadsheet, because I think I find it much easier to just review them overall that way.
So you can take a look at the full list of prompts in that spreadsheet, and we can add.
We ones.
We can make any adjustments that are needed as well.
So you'll see in the prompts, some are custom prompts that we've created, and some are based on kind of your categories.
And the nice thing about the category prompts, the real value there is when you want to add new prompts, you can add custom prompts, but you can also just select from a shared library.
And these, for the most part, will be pre-populated with historical data.
So instead of what you usually have to do, which is like wait a few days for it to start collecting data, these will be collecting data now.
So you should be able to see insights right away.

But you can also add custom prompts, and those will usually take, I'd say, around two days to start collecting data, unless it's something really specific, and then sometimes it can take, you know, up to a week.
Okay.
Okay.
And then once you-
So to sort of dig into the prompt itself, you'll see kind of the overall competitive positioning.
And then you can also get platform for specific, which is very useful.
So you can see where you're appearing for this prompt specifically across platforms.
Okay.
And then if you want to add additional competitors, you can do that from here.

So you can search, we track, I think we have five or 600, you know, sort of in this category and adjacent categories, but you can also add any competitor just with their URL and we'll start collecting data on that as well.
Okay.
The other useful thing, if you go into sources, is you're able to see the top cited content.
Once it loads, you'll be able to see the top cited content.
Kind of the stage of the B2B buyer journey.
So if you have specific things you're tracking for personas at different stages, it just makes it a little bit easier to follow those trends.
Same thing.
You can kind of group it by your personas that you're tracking and you can add tags as well as we kind of refine the strategy there.
And I think the last thing here would just be there's a section on your company context.
So this is all preloaded with the information that we collected when we started Sprint.
But like as you add new products and features, as your personas get updated, we can continue to update this section to give it more rich context about Cresta.
Okay.
Just for, how should we be using this compared to other reporting that we have within GrowthX?
Like I, I, this is the first I've heard of.
Check that.
So I just want to make sure that I understand like the full context here.
So basically this is just what we're using to build the AI visibility part of your AI strategy.
So the way that clients use it really depends on you.
Like some clients want to be in there.
They want to be editing their prompts.
There's things they want to track.
They just really want that visibility in the same way they want the SEO visibility, that reporting functions that we give on Looker.
Some clients are like very happy to have just understand that this is what we're using to kind of track your AI visibility and they don't want to be in the tool at all.
Right.
So this is more just it's a bonus that that comes, you know, with your engagement with us.

So if you want kind of access to that and want to be able to track it in the same way you have access to Looker to be able to kind of get a sense of your organic performance, this is the AI visibility part of that.
So there's no expectation, nothing that you need.
Need to be in here doing.

The only thing that I would suggest is, and I'll share the link in Slack after this as well, just to review the list of prompts to make sure that we are tracking the prompts that you care most about in the same way that you kind of, you really aligned on your keyword strategy.

Because the more we kind of front load those prompts in terms of like collecting data now, the more valuable it will be to make sure we're kind of tracking the personas, tracking the things you care about.
So I think taking a look at those and making sure they look good and we're not missing anything there, I think that would be the only real action item right now.
Okay.
Bailey, where would the invite have come from?
It should have come from us and it should be in your inbox, but I think we were just saying that it doesn't look like you got it.
It's kind of slowly heading, trending in the right direction, which is pretty normal, especially given, you know, we didn't publish last week.
So also heading in the right direction.

And one thing I had called out in a previous week is that I did dig a little bit into this best AI agents, platforms piece of content, just because I noticed that we had a huge number of impressions, but it was very low in clicks.
So I did pull the query list here and saw that most of the queries are these kind of natural language buyer questions that you see on LLM search.
So I would say that this piece of content is a great example of how, even though it's low clicks, I think what's happening is that it's being surfaced in this AI overview page.
So users are getting their answers without clicking.
So I think it's pulling that AEO visibility lever, even if it's not necessarily.
So I just wanted to flag that if you see that, we did dig into that, and I would say it's not a problem, it's just not necessarily being reflected in Looker there.
Okay.
Thank you.
I think that was it on my end.
And the only thing I wanted to flag with, and I know we had to cancel our product walkthrough a couple weeks ago, so I would love to get that back on the calendar whenever it works for you all.
So no rush there, but we're running.
How long should we plan for for that?
Let me look back and see.
We didn't have an agenda yet.
I think your team was just going to walk, do a little bit of a deeper dive into product to make sure we could layer that into our artifacts.
So I think I might remember if it was 30 minutes or an hour that had been.
Scheduled before.
Probably, I mean, it sort of depends on how deep we want to go.
I think the initial one we did was 30 minutes, so that's probably enough.
Was that one specifically, do you know if that was specifically with Oren?
Yeah, that one was with Oren.
Okay, so we'll need to probably set up a few specific ones.
Yeah, so I think that one, the goal was then to, we did that one specifically with Oren, so just to round out kind of our product view into the full landscape.
Okay, I can get that set up.
All right, thank you.
All right, I think that was it on Mayan.
Was there anything else that you all wanted to talk through?
Good.
I don't think so.
Okay, I will finish updating the table today, and hopefully we will be back on track with you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content pipeline, new AI visibility tool, and strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Pipeline Unblocked:** The 10-article backlog will be published immediately to accelerate progress. Nicky will update statuses in the new Airtable interface today.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=440.0)
  - [**Strategy Shift:** Re-prioritize low-volume "vs." and QM-focused articles to High/Medium. Rationale: They capture high-intent search and train LLMs to cite Cresta's products.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
  - [**New Tool Live:** GrowthX's AI visibility tool, CheckThat, is live. Nicky and Annie will receive new invites to review the 225 tracked prompts and ensure they align with Cresta's strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
  - [**Product Walkthrough:** A 30-min product deep-dive with Oren will be rescheduled to update GrowthX's internal knowledge base and inform future content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1641.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Prioritization

  - [**Backlog Publishing:** The 10-article backlog will be published immediately to accelerate progress.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=440.0)
      - [**Rationale:** No negative SEO impact from batch publishing this volume; the priority is catching up.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [**New Airtable Interface:** A new interface simplifies status updates. Nicky confirmed the team will use it to update the backlog today.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)
  - [**Topic Prioritization Review:** Revisit low-priority topics in Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
      - [**"Vs." Articles (e.g., Cresta vs. Competitor):**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
          - [**Action:** Re-prioritize to High.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Capture high-intent search and train LLMs to cite Cresta's products.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
      - [**QM-Focused Keywords (e.g., call center scoring):**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=661.0)
          - [**Action:** Re-prioritize to Medium.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=661.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Low-volume but problem-solving intent; directly showcases Cresta's automated scoring product.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=661.0)
  - [**Unindexed Pages:** A list of older, unindexed pages remains.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
      - [**Context:** Ada previously resubmitted these, so the current status may be a processing delay.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
      - [**Action:** Monitor the list to determine if further action is needed.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)

### CheckThat AI Visibility Tool

  - [**Overview:** GrowthX's new AI visibility tool, CheckThat, is live. It tracks Cresta's presence, sentiment, and share of citations in AI search results.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
  - [**Access & Usage:**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
      - [Nicky and Annie will receive new invites, as the first batch may have failed.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1004.0)
      - [**Action:** Review the 225 tracked prompts (via the shared spreadsheet) to ensure they align with Cresta's strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)
  - [**Key Features:**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1055.0)
      - [**Prompt Library:** Add new prompts from a shared library with historical data for immediate insights.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1143.0)
      - [**Platform-Specific Data:** View performance by AI platform (e.g., Google's AI Overview).](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
      - [**Source Analysis:** See top-cited content, grouped by buyer journey stage and persona.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1243.0)
      - [**Company Context:** Update Cresta's profile with new products/features to enrich the AI's understanding.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1342.0)

### Product Walkthrough

  - [**Goal:** Reschedule the canceled 30-min product deep-dive with Oren.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1641.0)
  - [**Purpose:** Update GrowthX's internal knowledge base to inform content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1641.0)

### Imagery Feedback

  - [**Action:** Bailey will create a Notion doc with the updated imagery pipeline for Steve's review.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Steve is the primary contact for design feedback, and a previous meeting on this topic may not have occurred.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Nicky:**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1733.0)
      - [Update statuses for the 10-article backlog in Airtable (due today).](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)
      - [Reschedule the 30-min product walkthrough with Oren.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1641.0)
  - [**Nicky & Annie:**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)
      - [Review the 225 CheckThat prompts (via spreadsheet) to confirm alignment with strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)
  - [**Bailey:**](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1021.0)
      - [Resend CheckThat invites to Nicky and Annie.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=1021.0)
      - [Create a Notion doc with the imagery pipeline for Steve's review.](https://fathom.video/share/PCXa4nt2hcLSXEWX1Fpt8_mUsx_-iUkc?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)

action_items.description: Review Airtable topics; approve/reject, add comments
Re-prioritize vs/comparison and QM scoring topics to Medium/High
Review non-indexed pages list; decide changes
Document image pipeline updates in Notion; tag Steve for feedback
Resend CheckThat invites to Nicky and Annie
Share CheckThat prompts sheet in Slack; request review from Nicky and Annie
